title: Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu Manuscript Witness Census

authors.name: Kazemipourleilabadi, Negar
authors.affiliation: Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München, Department of Art History

intro: A codicological inventory of manuscript witnesses to the Dīvān of Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu (d. 872 AH / 1467 CE; pen name Ḥaqīqī / Hakîkî), Qaraqoyunlu ruler-poet.
search_key_summary: Includes a search-key matrix covering modern Latin, 19th-c. Orientalist, Soviet-Cyrillic, Arabic-script, and catalogue-context forms across Persian, Turkish, Ottoman Turkish, and Azerbaijani Turkic, plus a script_families block of 9 additional script traditions added in v1.1.0 (Russian Cyrillic, Az Latin/Cyrillic, Georgian, Uzbek Latin/Cyrillic, Tajik Cyrillic, Devanagari, Urdu nastaʿliq); 5 tezkire entries plus 5 research-log extracts on Hakîkî (verbatim sample verses where available).
alevi_summary: The Alevi shrine corpus (Anatolian and Iranian Azerbaijani Ocak tradition) preserves eleven ghazals not present in the institutional editions examined, plus a complete unphotographed divan codex.
methodology_summary: Methodology: Witness census combining aggregator sweeps (~30 union catalogues), tezkire / reference extraction (10 extract files: 5 tezkire entries plus 5 research-log extracts), structured corpus probes (Teece 2016 Pir Budaq library), mecmûʿa adjacency mining, auction-house archives, scribe-name search, printed-catalogue OCR backchain, and modern editor backchain.
